name: oracle-ai-data-platform-workbench-databricks-migrator
description: Drive the Oracle AI Data Platform (AIDP) Databricks Migration Toolkit in natural language. Teaches Codex how to plan and execute an automated end-to-end migration of Databricks (Unity Catalog / HMS) notebooks, jobs, schedules, and catalog DDL onto AIDP — Pass-1 dependency resolution + Pass-2 cell-by-cell execute/verify/fix on a live AIDP cluster. Covers job_migrate.py orchestration, catalog migration (18 DDL rewrite rules: 3-part→2-part name flatten, s3://→oci:// via bucket-map, source-format preserved with delta.* property scrub), workflow DAG manifest building, fixup_cell rewind, pre-migration data-availability checks, write-redirect sandbox schema, and the acceptance-contract pattern for batch/streaming convergence. Bundles 16 skills + 5 reference docs covering the catalog DDL rules, common Databricks→AIDP gotchas (FUSE consistency, /Volumes mount, builtins.sum shadow, %run trailing slash, line-magic guard, Python 3.12 f-string strictness), JOB_REPORT.md parsing, and an env-coords scaffold customers fill in. Knowledge-only plugin — assumes the migrator source is checked out locally.

interface.shortDescription: Plan and execute automated Databricks → Oracle AIDP migrations from Codex.
interface.longDescription: Drive the Oracle AI Data Platform (AIDP) Databricks Migration Toolkit in natural language. Pass-1 rewrites Databricks APIs in every dependency notebook; Pass-2 executes each task cell-by-cell on a live AIDP cluster with 4-way verify (exception / stderr / Spark logs / model eval) and up to 10 fix attempts per cell. Handles Unity Catalog / HMS DDL via 18 deterministic rewrite rules (3-part→2-part flatten, s3://→oci://, format-preserving), workflow DAG manifest building, write-redirect sandbox schema for data safety, resume-from-cache, and an acceptance contract for streaming / batch convergence. Knowledge-only plugin — the actual migrator is a separate Python toolkit you clone locally.
defaultPrompt: Use aidp-migrator-overview to pick the right skill for the user's request — migrating a Databricks job, the Unity Catalog DDL, just the data check, or resuming a prior run.
Use aidp-migrator-bootstrap on first-time setup to confirm prerequisites: cloned migrator repo, ~/.oci/config, ACTIVE AIDP cluster, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, and the env-coords scaffold.
Use migrate-job-flow for the full guided end-to-end migration with checkpoints between phases.
